rule.desc: Use NamedTuple with type hints for simple immutable data structures.
rule.when_to_use: - NamedTuple: Simple, immutable data with few fields - dataclass: More complex data, needs methods, optional mutability - Plain tuple: Only for very short-lived, obvious positional data
rule.rationale: NamedTuples are immutable, lightweight, and have good performance. Better than plain tuples.

example.code:
# Good - typed NamedTuple class RecordData(NamedTuple): """Raw data record from input file.""" booking_date: date receipt_date: date amount: Decimal merchant: str location: str
# Usage booking = RecordData( booking_date=date(2025, 1, 15), receipt_date=date(2025, 1, 14), amount=Decimal("99.99"), merchant="Amazon", location="Online" )
# Immutable # booking.amount = Decimal("100.00") # AttributeError
# Good indexing and attributes print(booking[0]) # date(2025, 1, 15) print(booking.amount) # Decimal('99.99')
# Bad - plain tuple booking = ( date(2025, 1, 15), date(2025, 1, 14), Decimal("99.99"), "Amazon", "Online" ) # No attribute access, no type hints, unclear meaning

rule.tool_support: NamedTuple from `typing`. mypy checks field types.
